source_identity_policy: Wrapper libraries, mirrors, SDKs, and endpoint collections are not treated as primary publishers. Any formal data use must identify the original publisher, endpoint, license, snapshot rule, and revision/as-of semantics before D1/D2/D3 design can depend on it.

sources.raw_snapshot_retention_rule: Keep the downloaded official constituent file or captured page bytes under data/external with SHA-256, retrieval timestamp, source URL, and independent review handoff. Do not replace the G0 snapshot in place.
sources.as_of_policy: Use only the effective date stated or implied by the official constituent evidence. Do not infer historical index membership beyond DR-001 static-backfill boundaries.
sources.revision_policy: If CSI republishes or corrects a constituent document, register a new snapshot_id and keep the old bytes immutable.
sources.rate_limit_or_quota: Manual or low-frequency official document retrieval only; no automated bulk market-data crawling authorized.
sources.field_coverage_notes: Covers official CSI 800 membership evidence for G0. Does not by itself cover prices, trading status, corporate actions, or financial statements.
known_limitations: Approved only for the already reviewed G0 universe membership evidence chain.
Static current constituent cohort is not a historical membership history.

sources.raw_snapshot_retention_rule: If consulted, retain only repository commit hash, endpoint notes, and review notes. Do not store wrapper-returned data as formal raw snapshots unless the underlying original endpoint has its own registry entry and approval.
sources.as_of_policy: Repository commit time is only a tooling review timestamp, not a market-data observed_at time.
sources.revision_policy: Pin repository commit for endpoint research. Any endpoint promoted for formal use must receive its own source_registry_id and revision policy.
sources.rate_limit_or_quota: Not applicable for formal data use; downstream endpoint limits must be reviewed separately.
sources.field_coverage_notes: Useful for discovering A-share data endpoints and field names, but it is not the publisher of the underlying facts.
known_limitations: Cannot establish data license for underlying endpoints.
Cannot establish as-of semantics or revision policy for returned data.
Cannot be used as a formal data source without primary endpoint attribution.

sources.raw_snapshot_retention_rule: Every formal request must persist raw response bytes or vendor export files under data/raw or data/external with endpoint, parameters, API version if available, retrieval timestamp, account context, SHA-256, and manifest link. API keys and secrets must never be committed.
sources.as_of_policy: Blocked until the API documents whether each endpoint returns point-in-time, latest revised, or mixed histories. Formal use must record observed_at, endpoint timestamp, request parameters, and factor_as_of_time where applicable.
sources.revision_policy: Revision policy is blocked until full dump and incremental dump behavior is tested. If vendor data revises history, each dump must receive a data snapshot version and old snapshots remain immutable.
sources.rate_limit_or_quota: Pending account review; must document API key scope, quota, throttling, retry policy, full-market export limits, and vendor marketdb boundary before G4 authorization.
sources.field_coverage_notes: Candidate broad primary source for A-share master data, market data, corporate actions, reports, calendars, index data, abnormal limit-up data, dragon-tiger lists, and full-market export workflows.
known_limitations: Formal use is blocked until API key handling, license terms, quota, raw response retention, and revision semantics are reviewed.
Vendor-managed marketdb or local export cache must be treated as upstream/source cache, not as the project DuckDB warehouse.
Endpoint capability does not imply permission for research redistribution or committed raw data.

sources.raw_snapshot_retention_rule: Persist raw query result files with login client version, query parameters, adjustment flag, fields, retrieval timestamp, row count, and SHA-256. Do not rely on the remote service as the only copy.
sources.as_of_policy: Treat returned historical data as final-revised history until proven otherwise. Formal point-in-time use is blocked unless revision timing and adjustment factor as-of behavior are documented or empirically bounded.
sources.revision_policy: Run repeated snapshot comparisons before formal use. Any historical changes must produce a new source snapshot version with diff attribution.
sources.rate_limit_or_quota: No API key is required, but practical request throttling and service availability must be measured before any full-market pull.
sources.field_coverage_notes: Candidate backup or cross-check source for daily OHLCV, adjustment flags, suspension/trading status signals, valuation fields, and selected statements.
known_limitations: Free availability does not settle redistribution or formal research-use licensing.
As-of and revision semantics are not accepted yet.
Adjusted data cannot be used for D2 without explicit factor timing review.

sources.source_name: Public A-share web/API endpoint review bucket
sources.publisher_or_vendor: Multiple public website or API publishers
sources.raw_snapshot_retention_rule: Before any endpoint is used, create a dedicated source_registry_id with publisher identity, terms review, snapshot rules, hash requirements, and failure blocking criteria.
sources.field_coverage_notes: Category placeholder for later source candidates such as exchange, vendor, or public web endpoints. It does not authorize scraping or ingestion.
known_limitations: Cannot satisfy D1/D2/D3 source requirements without a later registry entry.

blocking_rules: No source may feed D1/D2/D3 until license_status and terms_review_status permit the specific allowed use.
Any source without raw_snapshot_required=true and hash_required=true is blocked from formal use.
Any wrapper or SDK must be traced to the underlying publisher and endpoint before returned data can be treated as raw evidence.
Any unknown as_of_policy or revision_policy blocks formal point-in-time research use.
Secrets, API keys, account tokens, and licensed raw data must not be committed.
